programmes.show_description: The Unseen Bits: The gags from the fact-or-fiction panel show that cruelly hit the cutting room floor get a second chance in this witty compilation special! Rob Brydon hosts.
Upbringing: Sandi Toksvig takes a look at upbringing with Sally Phillips, Josh Pugh, Aaron Simmonds and Alan Davies.
Stasis Leak: Interstellar comedy. Lister reads Rimmer's diary and discovers a leak from the 'Stasis' - a suspended-animation chamber - that creates a doorway to the past.
Holly Walsh, Josh Widdicombe, Adrian Chiles, Melvin Odoom and Gemma Collins are the celebrities enjoying a meal on the house - or so they hope - in this episode.
Steph McGovern, Russell Kane, Dotty, Steve Pemberton and Richard Madeley are the famous faces hoping to get a free lunch - well, dinner, really - in this edition.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Public Transport: Richard turns his attention to the noise, dirt and discomfort of public transport as Russell Howard joins him on London's Tube to assess the effectiveness of gadgets.
Puddings: More yummy treats from Blighty. The bikers create some classic puds and explore the history behind our favourites.
More motoring mayhem. The lads must each buy a car for under 1500 pounds and drive it across the unwieldy terrain in Botswana - and it can't be a 4x4!
The lads are on a mission to build a bridge over the River Kwai, but first they must drive across Burma - a nation largely closed to Westerners - in three rickety lorries!
The always fast and occasionally furious lads race the Mercedes SLS, the Porsche 911 GT3 RS and the Ferrari 458 Italia in an epic road trip up America's east coast.
In this special Christmas episode, Freddie, Chris and Paddy play 'car secret Santa' for a festive road trip across Britain transporting a giant Christmas tree to a winter wonderland.
Jack Carroll, Bridget Christie, Gabby Logan and Big Zuu join host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell to sort out the fact from the fiction.
Jessica Hynes, Romesh Ranganathan, Wilfred Webster and Gina Yashere join host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell to sort out the fact from the fiction.
Craig Charles, Amy Gledhill, Shazia Mirza and Jeremy Vine join host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell to sort out the fact from the fiction.
Sinitta, Jo Brand, Ivo Graham and Johnny Marr join host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell to sort out the fact from the fiction.
Babatunde Aleshe, Mike Bubbins, Jessica Knappett and Claudia Winkleman join host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell to sort out the fact from the fiction.
Sam Campbell, Will Mellor, Kimberley Walsh and Charlene White join host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell to sort out the fact from the fiction.
Trumped-up tales and uncomfortable truths in the humorous panel show! The little liars joining Rob Brydon and co are Matt Dawson, Mel Giedroyc, Josh Widdicombe and Dermot O'Leary.
The truth hurts, chiefly when you think it's a lie. David Mitchell, Lee Mack, Susan Calman, Richard Osman, Carol Kirkwood and David O'Doherty trade fibs in the witty panel show.
Rob Brydon presents the fact-or-fiction panel show, as David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by funny fibbers Bob Mortimer, Sarah Millican, Jon Richardson and David Harewood.
Stasis Leak: Interstellar comedy. Lister reads Rimmer's diary and discovers a leak from the 'Stasis' - a suspended-animation chamber - that creates a doorway to the past.
Queeg: The classic space comedy with a legion of fans. Holly's increasingly erratic behaviour endangers the ship and backup computer Queeg 500 is brought in to replace him.
Parallel Universe: The smegging brilliant space comedy! Holly devises a faster-than-light drive which immediately goes wrong and propels Red Dwarf into another universe.
Hot Tub: The gags come thick and fast in Lee Mack's much-loved sitcom. A game of 'hot tub fantasy date' lands Lee and Toby in very hot water with Emma Bunton and Susie Dent.
Charlie: Lee Mack is the big kid posing as a responsible adult. Lee worries that Charlie is getting a reputation for being the class clown. Wonder where he gets that from?
Marriage Guidance: The pun-filled odd couple comedy - well, one of them is odd anyway. Lucy thinks her and Lee need marriage counselling. Will a new, thoughtful Lee emerge?

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Guest host Steph McGovern, comedian Jamie MacDonald, and mathematician Professor Hannah Fry join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.
The brilliant Nabil Abdulrashid introduces Jake Lambert and Rachel Fairburn to the stage.
Ria Lina introduces comedians Lucy Beaumont and Josh Pugh to the stage.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
The Staycation: This episode features the world's tiniest caravan, 360-degree cameras, 300-litre rucksacks and a pair of trousers that transform into a picnic table.
The always fast and occasionally furious lads race the Mercedes SLS, the Porsche 911 GT3 RS and the Ferrari 458 Italia in an epic road trip up America's east coast.
Patriotic petrolheads Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May head on a trade mission to India, equipped with three old British cars and a range of uniquely British products.
The fun-packed petrolhead show hosted by Jeremy Clarkson and co. The chaps try to prove that a fly-drive holiday to Florida is more fun if you buy a car rather than rent one.
The controversial special of the motoring magazine that saw the boys chased out of Argentina! Jeremy, Richard and James drive three sports cars through spectacular Patagonia.
Trawlerman: In one of the most intense experiences of his life, Grimsby-based Guy Martin takes on the dangerous job of working as a deckhand on a fishing trawler.
Wildlife Warden: Guy gets to grips with nature as a wildlife warden on the remote Calf of Man island while trying his hand at birdwatching, rat catching, and cleaning up a beach for seals.
In the second leg of his journey, Simon Reeve travels down the Rocky Mountains and meets a former Silicon Valley executive who has envisaged a new future for the land in Montana.
In the third leg of his journey, Simon Reeve is in the United States and travelling down the length of California to explore the land of the giant redwoods and the West Coast.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Mantle: The suspicious demise of a woman at the cult-like Wiltshire retreat of a self-proclaimed 'healer' sparks a global investigation and a link to a death in Australia.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Ghoul: A routine visit by the RSPCA to a house in Chigwell in Essex uncovers an underground UK dog fighting gang operating on a global scale.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Guest host Steph McGovern, comedian Jamie MacDonald, and mathematician Professor Hannah Fry join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.
Revolutions: Sandi Toksvig examines some revolutionary ideas with Alan Davies, Gyles Brandreth, Susan Calman and Jessica Fostekew.
Kanye's Glasto set is pilloried by the regulars and guests Rob Beckett, Miles Jupp, Romesh Ranganathan and Tiff Stevenson, who also pitch ideas for Unlikely Greetings Cards.
Dara O Briain hosts the potent panel show that's a heady mocktail of biting satire and surreal stand-up. With Hugh Dennis, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones and Josh Widdicombe.
